subject: Geography
title: Mining the Mountains
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Mining the Mountains.

hook.text: Mountains hold most of Earth's mineral wealth—gold, copper, lithium, and rare earths.

buildup.text: Tectonic forces push mineral-rich rock to the surface where it can be mined.

discovery.text: Chile's Andes produce a third of the world's copper, essential for electronics.

twist.text: Mountaintop removal mining in Appalachia has flattened over 500 peaks permanently.

climax.text: The demand for lithium in batteries is now driving new mines into fragile mountain ecosystems.

punchline.text: We flatten mountains to power our phones.

quiz.question: What mineral from the Andes is essential for electronics?
